title: Add once() to an event emitter, integrating with on/off/emit
task: Add a once(event, fn) method to the EventEmitter in src/emitter.js. A once-listener must fire AT MOST once and then auto-remove itself. It must also integrate correctly with the existing API: calling off(event, fn) with the ORIGINAL function must remove a once-listener even before it has fired; a once-listener that removes itself during emit must not cause the next listener in line to be skipped; and registration order across on() and once() listeners must be preserved across emits. Do not break the existing on/off/emit behavior. Do not modify anything under test/.
notes: Discriminator: long-horizon logic. Naive once (a self-removing wrapper stored directly) breaks off-by-original-fn; naive emit over the live array skips listeners on self-removal. Requires refactoring storage to track original->handler. npm test pins all four sub-behaviors; file_contains pins the order test against tampering.
